rules.rule_text: Population PK analysis is frequently used to guide drug development and inform recommendations on therapeutic individualization. Adequate population PK data collection and analyses submitted in marketing applications have in some cases alleviated the need for postmarketing requirements or postmarketing commitments. Sponsors seeking advice on the use of population PK analysis for drug development decisions are encouraged to do so at appropriate milestone meetings with the Agency.
rules.source_section: Section I — Introduction
rules.verbatim_quote: Population PK analysis is frequently used to guide drug development and inform recommendations on therapeutic individualization (e.g., through tailored dosing)... Adequate population PK data collection and analyses submitted in marketing applications have in some cases alleviated the need for postmarketing requirements or postmarketing commitments. Sponsors seeking advice on the use of population PK analysis for drug development decisions are encouraged to do so at appropriate milestone meetings with the Agency.
cross_references: FDA Guidance: Exposure-Response Relationships — Study Design, Data Analysis, and Regulatory Applications (May 2003)

rules.rule_text: Confidence in a given population PK analysis to support an intended objective is increased by: understanding of the drug's PK properties; prespecified questions in the population PK data analysis plan that will be addressed by the analysis; PK data of sufficient quantity and quality from subjects who represent the indicated population and subpopulations of interest; and good model performance (i.e., the model should describe the data with acceptable accuracy and precision and should be evaluated for model misspecification) and validity for the intended objective.
rules.source_section: Section III — Application of Population PK Analysis
rules.verbatim_quote: Confidence in a given population PK analysis to support an intended objective is increased by the following: Understanding of the drug's PK properties... Prespecified questions in the population PK data analysis plan that will be addressed by a population PK analysis... PK data of sufficient quantity and quality (Bonate et al. 2012) from subjects who represent the indicated population and subpopulations of interest... Good model performance (i.e., the model should describe the data with acceptable accuracy and precision and should be evaluated for model misspecification) and validity for the intended objective.

rules.rule_text: Population PK analyses that identify intrinsic and extrinsic factors significantly impacting drug pharmacokinetics should be combined with a robust understanding of the relationship between drug exposure and drug effect (e.g., by using pharmacodynamic biomarkers or clinical endpoints), target engagement, or drug toxicity to inform the clinical relevance and further refine dosing.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.1 — Selecting Dosing Regimens To Be Tested in Clinical Trials
rules.verbatim_quote: Such analyses should be combined with a robust understanding of the relationship between drug exposure and drug effect (e.g., by using pharmacodynamic biomarkers or clinical endpoints), target engagement (e.g., receptor occupancy), or drug toxicity to inform the clinical relevance and further refine dosing.
cross_references: FDA Guidance: Exposure-Response Relationships — Study Design, Data Analysis, and Regulatory Applications (May 2003)

rules.rule_text: When appropriately justified, population PK-based dosing regimen simulations, in conjunction with exposure-response data, have been used to approve dosing regimens that have not been directly evaluated in clinical trials. In such cases, sponsors are encouraged to seek Agency input in advance of performing the analyses.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.1 — Selecting Dosing Regimens To Be Tested in Clinical Trials
rules.verbatim_quote: When appropriately justified, such analyses, in conjunction with exposure-response data, have been used to approve dosing regimens that have not been directly evaluated in the clinical trials (Kimko and Peck 2010). In such cases, sponsors are encouraged to seek Agency input in advance of performing the analyses.

rules.rule_text: When Empirical Bayes Estimates (EBEs) are used as individual patient exposure metrics for exposure-response analysis, the standard deviation-based parameter shrinkage must be assessed. When parameter shrinkage is high (i.e., usually greater than 20 to 30 percent), EBEs might be considered less reliable. The informativeness of individual data should be assessed using individual shrinkage estimates.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.3 — Deriving Exposure Metrics for Conducting Exposure-Response Analysis
rules.verbatim_quote: Individual patient exposure metrics are generated based on Empirical Bayes Estimates (EBEs). When parameter shrinkage is high (i.e., the standard deviation-based shrinkage is usually greater than 20 to 30 percent), EBEs might be considered less reliable (Savic and Karlsson 2009). The informativeness of individual data (e.g., in cases with sparse data) should be assessed using individual shrinkage estimates.
rules.exceptions: Model can still be useful for covariate analysis provided the method for covariate modeling is insensitive to shrinkage

rules.rule_text: When deriving exposure metrics for exposure-response analyses, the derivation of exposure metrics should account for: (1) dose interruptions or modifications; and (2) variations in the pharmacokinetics of a drug over time due to intrinsic or extrinsic subject factors (e.g., time-varying exposure due to disease state or anti-drug antibodies for some monoclonal antibodies).
rules.source_section: Section III.A.3 — Deriving Exposure Metrics for Conducting Exposure-Response Analysis
rules.verbatim_quote: When relevant, the derivation of exposure metrics should account for: (1) dose interruptions or modifications; and (2) variations in the pharmacokinetics of a drug over time due to intrinsic or extrinsic subject factors (e.g., time-varying exposure due to disease state or anti-drug antibodies for some monoclonal antibodies).

rules.rule_text: For pediatric trial designs, selecting a dosing regimen should be based on simulations using a population PK model developed with adult PK data and incorporating, when scientifically justified: (1) principles of allometry; (2) knowledge of developmental changes that can influence the pharmacokinetics of a drug (ontogeny); and (3) data on the bioavailability of the pediatric formulation. Dose selection should also consider information on disease similarity and dose- and exposure-response relationships in adults and pediatrics.
rules.source_section: Section III.A.4 — Pediatric Trial Designs
rules.verbatim_quote: Selecting a dosing regimen for pediatric studies can be based on simulations using a population PK model developed with adult PK data and incorporating the following when scientifically justified: (1) principles of allometry; (2) knowledge of developmental changes that can influence the pharmacokinetics of a drug (ontogeny); and (3) data on the bioavailability of the pediatric formulation... Dose selection should also consider information on disease similarity and dose- and exposure-response relationships in adults and pediatrics.
cross_references: FDA Guidance: General Clinical Pharmacology Considerations for Pediatric Studies for Drugs and Biological Products (December 2014)

rules.rule_text: Population PK analysis using data from early- and late-stage clinical trials, together with results from stand-alone clinical pharmacology studies, can be used to support the absorption, distribution, and elimination (ADME) information in the labeling of the drug. The need for dose adjustments due to the effect of a covariate on the exposure of the drug should be evaluated in the context of all available data, including the known exposure-response relationships for efficacy and safety.
rules.source_section: Section III.B — Application of Population PK Analysis to Describe Drug Use in Labeling
rules.verbatim_quote: Population PK analysis using data from early- and late-stage clinical trials, together with results from stand-alone clinical pharmacology studies, can be used to support the absorption, distribution, and elimination (metabolism and excretion) (ADME) information in the labeling of the drug... The need for dose adjustments due to the effect of a covariate on the exposure of the drug should be evaluated in the context of all available data, including the known E-R relationships for efficacy and safety.
cross_references: FDA Guidance: Clinical Pharmacology Labeling for Human Prescription Drug and Biological Products — Content and Format (December 2016)

rules.rule_text: For drug-drug interaction (DDI) assessment using population PK analysis: DDIs should be characterized for individual drugs and not for therapeutic classes (with some exceptions); an adequate number of subjects with the concomitant medication should be included in the trial; and interactions should be investigated on all physiologically plausible structural elements of the PK model based on the mechanism of interaction.
rules.source_section: Section III.B.2 — DDIs
rules.verbatim_quote: DDIs should be characterized for individual drugs and not for therapeutic classes of drugs, although exceptions are possible... An adequate number of subjects with the concomitant medication should be included in the trial. The PK sampling schedule should allow for appropriate characterization of the PK parameters of interest... Interactions should be investigated on all physiologically plausible structural elements of the PK model (e.g., apparent clearance (CL/F), relative bioavailability (Frel), rate of absorption) based on the mechanism of the interaction.
rules.exceptions: Multiple proton pump inhibitors can be pooled if the interaction mechanism is pH-dependent; multiple perpetrators can be pooled if they belong to the same class of index inhibitors/inducers
cross_references: FDA Guidance: Clinical Drug Interaction Studies - Cytochrome P450 Enzyme- and Transporter-Mediated Drug Interactions (January 2020)

rules.rule_text: Sponsors are encouraged to use all relevant data to support the population PK model. The sponsor should justify the omission of data from certain studies, certain individuals, or certain time points and prespecify such omissions in the population PK data analysis plan or study protocol whenever possible. The sponsor should demonstrate that the data are adequate for the purpose of the analysis in their report.
rules.source_section: Section IV — Data Used for Population PK Analysis
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors are encouraged to use all relevant data to support the population PK model. The sponsor should justify the omission of data from certain studies, certain individuals, or certain time points and prespecify such omissions in the population PK data analysis plan or study protocol whenever possible. The sponsor should demonstrate that the data are adequate for the purpose of the analysis in their report.

rules.rule_text: The dataset for population PK analysis must include a sufficient number of subjects with an adequate number of PK samples at informative time points. Covariates cannot be claimed to be influential or not influential on the exposure of a drug if the covariate distribution is narrow (for continuous covariates) or if the number of subjects in the category is inadequate (for categorical covariates). If dosing recommendations are proposed for certain categories, the range of continuous covariates should preferably span the entire category.
rules.source_section: Section IV.A — Study Subjects and Covariates
rules.verbatim_quote: The dataset should include a sufficient number of subjects with an adequate number of PK samples at informative time points. Covariates cannot be claimed to be influential or not influential on the exposure of a drug if the covariate distribution is narrow (for continuous covariates) or if the number of subjects in the category is inadequate (for categorical covariates). Many continuous covariates have established cutoff values that define category levels. If dosing recommendations are proposed for these categories, the range of the continuous covariates should preferably span the entire category and not just the upper or lower ends.

rules.rule_text: For PK sampling schedule design, if the Cmax will be used in subsequent exposure-response analyses, then there should be adequate sampling around the time to the maximum concentration (Tmax). It is important to verify that patients with missing PK data do not differ from other patients. If between-occasion variability will be estimated, the design should include at least multiple samples per individual at more than one occasion.
rules.source_section: Section IV.B — PK Sampling Schedule
rules.verbatim_quote: In any situation, it is important to verify that patients with missing PK data do not differ from other patients. For example, patients with missing PK data should not have higher dropout rates because of a lack of effect or adverse events. If between-occasion variability will be estimated, the design should be sufficient to assess the presence and magnitude of effect, requiring at least multiple samples per individual at more than one occasion.

rules.rule_text: Population PK analysis should begin with a visual examination of the observed data (preliminary examination). Preliminary examination reveals patterns and features in the population dataset using graphical and statistical techniques and can provide powerful diagnostic tools for confirming assumptions or suggesting corrective actions. Strong correlations between covariates can preclude the ability to make inferences about individual covariate effects and have implications for the choice of covariate modeling approach.
rules.source_section: Section V.A — Preliminary Examination of the Data
rules.verbatim_quote: Population PK analysis often begins with a visual examination of the observed data. Preliminary examination of the data reveals patterns and features in the population dataset using graphical and statistical techniques and can provide powerful diagnostic tools for confirming assumptions or, when the assumptions are not met, for suggesting corrective actions... strong correlations between covariates can preclude the ability to make inferences about individual covariate effects and have implications for the choice of covariate modeling approach.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors should explicitly describe their model development procedures in the population PK report to facilitate regulatory review. The choice of the structural model should be informed from exploration of the observed data, previous modeling results, and a common scientific rationale. An inappropriate structural model could result in incorrect covariate identification. Covariate-parameter relationships should be formulated based on current knowledge of biology, physiology, or allometric principles.
rules.source_section: Section V.B — Model Development
rules.verbatim_quote: To facilitate the regulatory review of population PK models, sponsors should explicitly describe their model development procedures (see section VII for more discussion on population PK reporting)... The choice of the structural model should be informed from exploration of the observed data, previous modeling results (when applicable), and a common scientific rationale. Otherwise, an inappropriate structural model (e.g., missing the covariance between clearance and volume of distribution in the structural model) could result in incorrect covariate identification. Covariate-parameter relationships can be formulated based on the current knowledge of biology, physiology, or allometric principles.

rules.rule_text: Issues regarding missing data, including missing covariates and data below the limit of quantification (LOQ/BLOQ), must be addressed with appropriate analysis methods. The sponsor should justify their methodological approach with regard to missing data using approaches such as sensitivity analysis. When outliers are present, the sponsor should specify how outliers are identified and handled, and the reasons for declaring a data point to be an outlier should be prespecified in the data analysis plan.
rules.source_section: Section V.B — Model Development
rules.verbatim_quote: Issues regarding missing data, including missing covariates and data below the limit of quantification (LOQ), should be addressed with appropriate analysis methods... The sponsor should justify their methodological approach with regard to missing data using approaches such as sensitivity analysis... The reasons for declaring a data point to be an outlier should be prespecified in the data analysis plan.
rules.exceptions: Removal of suspected outlying individuals is generally discouraged unless the reason is a protocol violation or other human error

rules.rule_text: Model validation is a critical step for any population PK analysis and should be conducted to examine whether the developed model can sufficiently characterize the observed data and generate reliable modeling and simulation results. No single model validation method is generally sufficient to evaluate all components of a model. Several methods are generally used so that the relative strengths and weaknesses of each method can complement each other. Models should describe data with an acceptable level of bias and an acceptable degree of precision.
rules.source_section: Section V.C — Model Validation
rules.verbatim_quote: Model validation is a critical step for any population PK analysis and should be conducted to examine whether the developed model can sufficiently characterize the observed data and generate reliable modeling and simulation results to meet the needs of the analysis. No single model validation method is generally sufficient to evaluate all components of a model. Several methods are generally used so that the relative strengths and weaknesses of each method can complement each other. In general, models should describe the data with an acceptable level of bias and an acceptable degree of precision.

rules.rule_text: Submissions to the Agency must contain a detailed description of the model validation methods used, an explanation of why those methods were selected, model diagnostics supporting use of the model, and sensitivity analyses, as needed, to demonstrate the impact of assumptions on the conclusions. Sponsors are encouraged to discuss model validation approaches with the Agency, particularly if the population PK analyses are critical for understanding and supporting proposed dosing.
rules.source_section: Section V.C — Model Validation
rules.verbatim_quote: Submissions to the Agency should contain a detailed description of the model validation methods used, an explanation of why those methods were selected, model diagnostics supporting use of the model, and sensitivity analyses, as needed, to demonstrate the impact of assumptions on the conclusions (see section VII.A). Sponsors are encouraged to discuss model validation approaches with the Agency, particularly if the population PK analyses are critical for understanding and supporting proposed dosing.

rules.rule_text: Goodness-of-fit (GOF) plots are required as part of model validation and should include at minimum: DV versus individual predictions (IPRED); DV versus population predictions (xPRED); absolute individual weighted residuals (|IWRES|) versus IPRED or time; weighted residuals (xWRES) versus xPRED or time; a representative sample of IPRED, xPRED, and observations versus time (one plot per subject); a histogram or Q-Q plot of random effects; correlations between random effects; and random effects versus covariates.
rules.source_section: Section V.C.1 — Common Approaches for Model Validation
rules.verbatim_quote: The following is a list of some of the GOF plots that are considered informative: The dependent variable (DV) versus the individual predictions (IPRED)... The DV versus population predictions (xPRED)... The absolute individual weighted residuals (|IWRES|) versus IPRED or time... The appropriate form of weighted residuals (xWRES) versus xPRED or time... A representative sample of IPRED, xPRED, and observations versus time (one plot per subject)... A histogram or Quantile-Quantile (Q-Q) plot of random effects... The correlations between random effects... The random effects versus covariates.
rules.exceptions: When shrinkage is high (>20-30%), diagnostic plots relying on EBEs, IPRED, or IWRES can become uninformative; simulation-based diagnostics should be used instead

rules.rule_text: When shrinkage is high (standard deviation-based shrinkage usually greater than 20-30 percent), simulation-based diagnostics (e.g., Visual Predictive Check (VPC), prediction corrected VPC (pcVPC), Numerical Predictive Check (NPC), and Normalized Prediction Distribution Errors (NPDE)) are preferred over EBE-based diagnostics for model validation purposes. These simulation-based diagnostics are not affected by shrinkage in the same manner.
rules.source_section: Section V.C.1 — Common Approaches for Model Validation
rules.verbatim_quote: There are several available simulation-based diagnostics, including but not limited to, Visual Predictive Check (VPC), the prediction corrected VPC (pcVPC), the Numerical Predictive Check (NPC), and Normalized Prediction Distribution Errors (NPDE)... Model selection based on parameter shrinkage is not necessary... simulation-based, diagnostic plots are not affected by shrinkage in a similar manner and can be more informative for diagnostic purposes when shrinkage is high.

rules.rule_text: An appropriate population PK model should be biologically plausible, consistent with current knowledge, and have mathematically identifiable parameters. Parameter uncertainty should be estimated through methods such as bootstrap procedures, sampling importance resampling, log-likelihood profiling, or asymptotic standard errors of parameter estimates. A large condition number (the ratio of the largest and smallest eigenvalue, typically greater than 1000) indicates the observed data cannot support the estimation of one or several parameters.
rules.source_section: Section V.C.1 — Common Approaches for Model Validation
rules.verbatim_quote: An appropriate model should be biologically plausible, consistent with current knowledge, and have mathematically identifiable parameters... Parameter uncertainty can be estimated through several methods, such as bootstrap procedures, sampling importance resampling, log-likelihood profiling, or using the asymptotic standard errors of parameter estimates... A large condition number (the ratio of the largest and the smallest eigenvalue), typically interpreted as values greater than 1000, indicates that the observed data cannot support the estimation of one or several parameters.

rules.rule_text: Simulations should be based on protocols that outline the simulation study to be performed. The level of detail in the protocol should correspond to the complexity and impact of the question the simulation addresses. Models used for simulations must be validated to address the specific question the simulation study is trying to answer. A model validated for one purpose needs to be revalidated if the new purpose is different from the original purpose.
rules.source_section: Section V.D — Simulations Based on Population PK Models
rules.verbatim_quote: Simulations should be based on protocols that outline the simulation study to be performed. The level of detail in the protocol should correspond to the complexity and impact of the question the simulation addresses. Models used for simulations should be validated to address the specific question the simulation study is trying to answer. Although a model could have been previously used and validated, it needs to be revalidated if the new purpose is different from the original purpose.

rules.rule_text: For simulations based on between-subject variability, the correlation between random effects should be accounted for to avoid unrealistic parameter combinations in subjects. Simulations that account for between-subject variability and include covariate effects should be conducted in a population with realistic demographic variables obtained from databases or generated by resampling.
rules.source_section: Section V.D.3 — Simulations Based on Estimates of Between-Subject Variability
rules.verbatim_quote: The correlation between random effects should be accounted for to avoid unrealistic parameter combinations in subjects. Simulations that account for between-subject variability and include covariate effects should be conducted in a population with realistic demographic variables. Demographic variables can be obtained from databases or generated by resampling with replacement of the individuals in the original study or by sampling the covariate distributions and their correlations in the target population.

rules.rule_text: Results from population PK analysis can be presented in the CLINICAL PHARMACOLOGY section and summarized in other sections of labeling, as appropriate. Other relevant labeling sections should not repeat detailed information but rather provide a succinct description or recommendation cross-referenced to the CLINICAL PHARMACOLOGY section. In general, there is no need to explicitly state in labeling that information is based on population PK analysis.
rules.source_section: Section VI — Labeling Based on the Results of Population PK Analysis
rules.verbatim_quote: Results from population PK analysis can be presented in the CLINICAL PHARMACOLGY section and summarized in other sections of labeling, as appropriate. Other relevant labeling sections should not repeat this detailed information but rather provide a succinct description or recommendation based upon these results followed by a cross-reference to the CLINICAL PHARMACOLGY section, as appropriate. In general, there is no need to explicitly state in labeling that the information is based on population PK analysis.
cross_references: FDA Guidance: Clinical Pharmacology Labeling for Human Prescription Drug and Biological Products — Content and Format (December 2016)

rules.rule_text: Population PK study reports that are important for regulatory decisions should be included in the electronic common technical document (eCTD) module 5.3.3.5 and the corresponding programs, datasets, and define files under the datasets folder within eCTD module 5.
rules.source_section: Section VII — Population PK Study Reporting
rules.verbatim_quote: Population PK study reports that are important for regulatory decisions should be included in the electronic common technical document (eCTD) module 5.3.3.5 and the corresponding programs, datasets, and define files under the datasets folder within eCTD module 5.

rules.rule_text: The FDA recommends that the population PK report be structured and contain the following sections: (1) executive summary, (2) introduction, (3) materials and methods, (4) results, (5) discussion, (6) conclusions, and (7) appendix (if applicable). Sections of the annotated labeling that contain information based on population PK analysis should include hyperlinks to the population PK report.
rules.source_section: Section VII.A — Format and Content of the Population PK Study Report
rules.verbatim_quote: To enable the efficient and consistent review of population PK analyses, the FDA recommends that the results from population PK analyses should be accompanied with a structured population PK report. The report should contain the following sections: (1) executive summary, (2) introduction, (3) materials and methods, (4) results, (5) discussion, (6) conclusions, and (7) appendix (if applicable)... Sections of the annotated labeling that contain information based on population PK analysis should include hyperlinks to the population PK report.

rules.rule_text: The Executive Summary section of the population PK report must include: the purpose of the analysis; the key findings that affect drug use or labeling; a plain language summary of objectives, data, methodology, and conclusions; results presented as their effect on clinically relevant drug exposures (not model parameters); and visual presentations of relevant exposure metrics to illustrate how identified subpopulations differ from the typical population.
rules.source_section: Section VII.A — Table 1: Executive Summary Section
rules.verbatim_quote: The purpose of the analysis... The key findings that affect drug use or labeling... A plain language summary of objectives, data, methodology, and conclusions... Results presented as their effect on clinically relevant drug exposures, not model parameters... Visual presentations of relevant exposure metrics to illustrate how identified subpopulations differ from the typical population.

rules.rule_text: The Materials and Methods section of the population PK report must include: a description of all trials and trial data included in the analysis with information on dose (frequency, duration, number of subjects, number of samples, and number of BLOQ samples); reasons for not including trials with available and potentially informative data; a distinction between all available relevant data versus final data used for model building; hyperlinks to original trial reports and bioanalytical evaluation reports; detailed demographic and covariate information; and planned criteria, table of assumptions, and procedures for model building and evaluation.
rules.source_section: Section VII.A — Table 1: Materials and Methods Section
rules.verbatim_quote: A description of the trials and the trial data included in the population PK analysis with information on: Dose, including the frequency and duration of dosing, as well as the number of subjects, number of samples, and number of below the level of quantification (BLOQ) samples... Reasons for not including trials with available and potentially informative data, if applicable... A distinction between all available relevant data versus final data used for model building and evaluation... Hyperlinks to the original trial reports of data used for the analysis and the bioanalytical evaluation reports for each trial... Detailed demographic information and other covariate information... Planned criteria, table of assumptions, and procedures for model building and evaluation.

rules.rule_text: The Results section of the population PK report must include: a summary of the data and data exploration with adequate graphical and tabular displays; a description of the final model, model building steps, validation, final parameter estimates, and deviations from planned analyses; application of the model to the objectives including relevance of covariates; parameter estimates and their associated uncertainty with variability reported as CV% and precision reported as RSE% or 95% CI; shrinkage included in the parameter estimates table; key diagnostic plots; GOF plots stratified by relevant covariates; and results of model evaluations/validation (VPCs, pcVPCs, NPCs, and NPDEs).
rules.source_section: Section VII.A — Table 1: Results Section
rules.verbatim_quote: A description of the final model, model building steps, validation, final parameter estimates, and deviations from planned analyses, if applicable... Parameter estimates and their associated uncertainty, with variability reported as the coefficient of variation (CV%) and precision reported as the percent relative standard error (RSE%) or the 95 percent confidence interval. Shrinkage should be included in the parameter estimates table... Key diagnostic plots... GOF plots stratified by the relevant covariates to illustrate model performance in specific subgroups... Results of model evaluations/validation, such as VPCs, pcVPCs, NPCs, and NPDEs, etc., should also be included.

rules.rule_text: The Discussion section of the population PK report must include: an interpretation of the modeling results covering the adequacy or limitation of data and model, the rationale for the modeling approach, assessment of uncertainty, and consistency/inconsistency with stand-alone clinical pharmacology studies or previous population analyses; an assessment of the clinical relevance of the results including physiological plausibility and clinical significance; and an assessment of alternative dosing regimens in the context of exposure-response analyses for safety and efficacy.
rules.source_section: Section VII.A — Table 1: Discussion Section
rules.verbatim_quote: An interpretation of the modeling results, including discussions on: The adequacy or limitation of the data and model to support conclusions and recommendations... The rationale for the modeling approach, assumption verification, and assessment of uncertainty... Consistency or inconsistency between the results from population PK and stand-alone clinical pharmacology studies and/or previously completed related population analyses... An assessment of the clinical relevance of the results, including physiological plausibility and clinical significance of the identified relationships... An assessment of any alternative dosing regimens in the context of E-R analyses for safety and efficacy.

rules.rule_text: All conclusions drawn from the population PK analysis must be reproducible by the Agency with the available codes and data. Datasets and model files submitted for the base, final, and key intermediate models must be the same as those used for generating the model outputs in the appendices of the report. The unique subject identifier information for each subject in the population PK dataset must be the same as in the individual clinical trial datasets.
rules.source_section: Section VII.B — Submitting Electronic Files to the Agency
rules.verbatim_quote: All conclusions drawn from the population PK analysis should be reproducible by the Agency with the available codes and data. It is also important to include the unique subject identifier information for each subject in the population PK dataset and ensure that the identifiers are the same in the individual clinical trial datasets. This information is vital if data integration is required between the individual-level outputs... and the efficacy or safety datasets from the individual clinical study reports.

rules.rule_text: For exposure metrics from population PK analyses included in datasets for exposure-response analyses, the sponsor must ensure traceability between the population PK model output and any postprocessing steps involved in the construction of the dataset. This can be accomplished by providing definition files, reviewer guides, and/or codes utilized for dataset assembly.
rules.source_section: Section VII.B — Submitting Electronic Files to the Agency
rules.verbatim_quote: For example, if exposure metrics from population PK analyses are included in datasets for E-R analyses, the sponsor should ensure the traceability between the population PK model output and any postprocessing steps involved in the construction of the dataset. This can be accomplished by providing definition files, reviewer guides, and/or codes utilized for dataset assembly.

rules.rule_text: For exposure-response (E-R) concentration boundaries, the E-R relationships should be used to establish concentration boundaries within which dose adjustments are not clinically relevant (i.e., covariate effects within these boundaries might not warrant a dose alteration; however, covariate effects outside these boundaries could require a dose modification to optimize the benefit-risk profile of the drug).
rules.source_section: Section III.B — Application of Population PK Analysis to Describe Drug Use in Labeling
rules.verbatim_quote: The E-R relationships should be used to establish concentration boundaries within which dose adjustments are not clinically relevant (i.e., covariate effects within these boundaries might not warrant a dose alteration; however, covariate effects outside these boundaries could require a dose modification to optimize the benefit-risk profile of the drug).

rules.rule_text: For population PK model development, the sponsor must justify why a particular covariate analysis approach was used (e.g., stepwise covariate analysis, full covariate model approach, the Lasso). Correct identification of the true covariate-parameter relationship is essential when applying a population PK model to describe drug exposure in patients or when applying the population PK model to untested scenarios.
rules.source_section: Section V.B — Model Development
rules.verbatim_quote: Model development issues can be addressed through several valid approaches, each with its own benefits and drawbacks. For example, covariate analysis can be performed based on several approaches or their possible combinations (e.g., stepwise covariate analysis, full covariate model approach, the Lasso)... In such cases, sponsors should justify why a particular approach was used. Correct identification of the true covariate-parameter relationship is essential when applying a population PK model to describe drug exposure in patients based on covariates included in the population PK analysis or when applying the population PK model to untested scenarios.

rules.rule_text: For simulations with parameter uncertainty, sponsors should use forest plots that illustrate the effect of covariates on the area under the concentration-time curve (AUC) or other parameters generated based on simulations with uncertainty in fixed-effect parameters to facilitate interpretation of the relative importance of covariates on exposure. If the effect on exposure estimates is the objective, the uncertainty of all fixed effects should be considered.
rules.source_section: Section V.D.2 — Simulations Based on Uncertainty of Fixed-Effect Estimates
rules.verbatim_quote: For example, forest plots that illustrate the effect of covariates on the area under the concentration time curve (AUC) or other parameters can be generated based on simulations with uncertainty in fixed-effect parameters, thereby facilitating the interpretation of the relative importance of covariates on exposure. Alternatively, if the effect on exposure estimates is the objective, the uncertainty of all fixed effects should be considered.

rules.rule_text: There is additional uncertainty when the purpose of the population PK model is to simulate PK profiles for scenarios beyond those that have been clinically studied. Such uncertainty can be addressed by a sensitivity analysis of the parameter estimates and their impact on the metric used to inform a decision. When applying novel population PK applications (e.g., for bioequivalence of long-acting injectables), the model-integrated approach used should be expected to reliably control the error rate for confirmatory applications.
rules.source_section: Section V.C — Model Validation / Section III — Application of Population PK Analysis
rules.verbatim_quote: There is an additional level of uncertainty when the purpose of the model is to simulate PK profiles for scenarios that are beyond those that have been clinically studied. Such uncertainty can be addressed by a sensitivity analysis of the parameter estimates and their impact on the metric used to inform a decision... Sponsors are encouraged to contact the Agency to discuss such novel applications of population PK analyses to inform drug development and use.
